description: 6 - 21-12-2024 - Emails between Robert Tinline (Director Americas, FCDO) and others
text:
From: Robert Tinline[ Personal
Sent: Sat 21/12/2024 6:35:39 PM (UTC)
To: JCS Nicholas
Alton Personal ; lain Griffiths Personal
James Roscoe Personal JCS
Ce: Mungo Woodifield Personal ; Lucy
Ferguson Personal Jude Muxworthy
versonal . JCS
Subject: Re: USCAD contact

Mandelson thinks that as a Privy Councillor he can see things without waiting for vetting.
Though whether he can in Personal and whether we agree he can not clear. To sort out on
Monday. R

Rob Tinline

Director Americas

Foreign. Commonwealth and Development Office
Personal | Personal

From: JCS

Sent: Saturday, December 21, 2024 6:26:49 PM

To: Nicholas Alton ; lain Griffiths ; James Roscoe; JCS

Cc: Robert Tinline ; Mungo Woodifield ; Lucy Ferguson ; Jude Muxworthy*; = JCS
Subject: RE: USCAD contact

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

lain/Nick —
PSB the different buckets we propose sending papers under. We will have a fair
amount on the stocks which | can start sending across on Monday.

‘S —do we have a rough timeframe for Peter's vetting/clearance? Helpful to know
where we are at now so we can vet material accordingly.
Political/Congressional/Economic:

+ US politics scene-setter

+ Who's who in the incoming Trump Administration [list of personalities and bios]
+ How to best work with Trump and lessons learned from the first Trump
Administration

* Congress 101 [including bios of congressional leadership]

+ US economy overview [including expected economic policy of incoming Trump
Admin and latest US economy quarterly report as annex]

* State of the Democratic Party

+ Subnational engagement [overview of the consulates, our subnational
engagement, key organisations and figures]

• Overview of US media landscape
• Overview of main DC-based think tanks
Trade and Global Issues:
• Trade and tariffs
• Economic security
• AI and emerging tech
• Science and research cooperation
• Energy, climate and environment
• Agriculture
• Transport
• Business engagement
Foreign and Security policy:
• Middle East - Israel/Gaza, Syria, Normalisation
• Iran
• Russia/Ukraine
• Development, the UN and Human Rights
• China and Indo-Pacific, including DPRK
• The Americas
• Euroatlantic security
• Sanctions
• Cyber
• BIOT
• Justice and Home Affairs
• Illicit finance
• Counter-terrorism
• Special cases, privileges and immunities
Best wishes,
 | Chief of Staff to His Majesty’s Ambassador | British Embassy 
Washington
3100 Massachusetts Avenue NW, Washington, DC 20008 | Tel: 
 | Mobile: 
 | Email: 
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
From: Nicholas Alton 
Sent: 21 December 2024 13:00
To: Iain Griffiths ; James Roscoe ; 
 
Cc: Robert Tinline ; Mungo Woodifield ; Lucy Ferguson ;
 ; Jude Muxworthy * ; 
Subject: Re: USCAD contact
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
Just to follow up on this after we spoke yesterday. We’ll pull together what we can on 
Monday. You should now have our list of suggested topics from 
? If not, let me 
know and I’ll try and send through.
Personal
Personal
Personal
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
12


Would you/USCAD be able to pull together as much as possible in tens of documents 
from that list from what you have on the stocks in London first thing on Monday? If 
you send over what you have by around 2pm your time, then we can pick up from 
here to fill in the gaps. That would help make the most of the time difference to max 
what we can do on Monday.
Many thanks
Nick
Nicholas Alton
Minister-Counsellor (Political)
British Embassy Washington
Cell:
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
From: Nicholas Alton 
Sent: Friday, December 20, 2024 4:03:39 PM
To: Iain Griffiths 
; James Roscoe 
; 
Cc: Robert Tinline 
; Mungo Woodifield 
; Lucy Ferguson
 
 
 Jude Muxworthy * 
; 
Subject: RE: USCAD contact
Hi Iain
James asked me to reply to you on this. I haven't seen the separate emailing so am coming 
to this a bit blind but we were working on the basis that 
 would send you a list of topics 
that we will cover today. I wasn't aware of the tight deadline for the wider pieces of work. 
When we discussed with James yesterday, we were working toward early January for those 
(although I understand that we actually need to pull this together for 27 December?).
If we actually now need to pull all this together for Monday, then I can have a go at some of it 
over the weekend (realistically I could probably pull together some existing reporting; and an 
initial list of US contacts). 
Nick
Nicholas Alton | Minister Counsellor (Political) | British Embassy Washington 
3100 Massachusetts Avenue NW | Washington DC 20008 
Cell: 
 | Phone:
 | 
From: Iain Griffiths 
Sent: Friday, December 20, 2024 1:03 PM
To: James Roscoe 
; 
Cc: Robert Tinline
 Mungo Woodifield 
; 
 Nicholas 
Alton 
;
 Jude 
Muxworthy * 
; 
Subject: RE: USCAD contact
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
James,
You'll have seen Rob's separate email outlining what we'll package up for 
HMA-
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
Personal
JCS
JCS
Personal
Personal
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
Personal
JCS
13


des at the beginning of next week. Given his request for a fairly substantive
pack of papers, having the set of files you set out below in a form presentable
for HMA-des (covering BEW policy products, London contacts, proposed US
networking and media engagement plan) by cop today would be a big help,
alongside the corporate pack Rob proposed in his email.

Best,

lain

lain Griffiths (he/him) | Head of US & Canada Department | Foreign,
Commonwealth & Development Office |

Email: Personal |Mobile: Personal |
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
From: James Roscoe Personal
Sent: 19 December 2024 16:52
To: lain Griffiths Personal z JCS
Ce: Robert Tinline Personal : Munao Woodifield
Personal - JCS Nicholas
Alton Personal 7 JUS Jude
Muxworthy * Personal : JS

Subject: RE: USCAD contact

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
JCS,
Delighted to meet you.
Enormous thanks for stepping up the plate on this.
We will provide you shortly with:
* taxonomy of policy issues we will want the candidate to read in on
* list of London contacts we would like the candidate to meet
* proposed US networking — including sequencing and format for meetings (individual,
meals, receptions etc)
* proposed US media engagement but in advance and on arrival.

Copying in the senior team here who'll be providing this.
And let’s speak— Personal

James
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
From: Robert Tinline Personal
Sent: 19 December 2024 11:22
To: Philip R Barton Personal Ailsa Terry (No.10)
Personal ; Caroline Hurndall Personal
Rochelle Fisher Personal + lain Griffiths
Personal ; James Roscoe Personal -JCS
JCS >, JCS

Personal
Subject: USCAD contact

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE


JCS (copied) will be the primary USCAD contact for HMA-des Washington.
Rob

Rob Tinline

Director Americas

Foreian. Commonwealth and Development Office
Personal | Personal

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
parsing_notes: Extract structured email headers (From, To, Cc, Subject, Date/Sent) for each message in the chain. Identify reply/forward nesting depth. Parse each message body separately. Redacted addresses appear as 'PERSONAL' — names are retained. Build sender→recipient edges per message for communication graph.
